parents.description: A practical overview of Lore and the basic workflow for agent memory.
parents.title: Introduction

children.title: What Lore is for
children.description: Learn which facts, preferences, decisions, and rules should become durable memory.

children.title: How Lore works
children.description: Understand the daily loop: boot context, recall, reading memories, and writing durable changes.

children.title: When to use it
children.description: Decide which information is worth saving and which short-lived notes can stay out of memory.

parents.description: Learn the core memory concepts: paths, domains, priority, disclosure, glossary terms, and healthy memory shape.
parents.title: Memory Basics

children.title: Memory paths
children.description: Use stable memory paths and domains to keep memory easy to browse, link, and move.

children.title: Disclosure triggers
children.description: Write concrete triggers so Lore knows when a memory should appear.

children.title: Priority levels
children.description: Use priority levels to mark importance without making recall noisy.

children.title: Glossary terms
children.description: Add product names, aliases, and acronyms that help recall find the right memory.

parents.description: Use recall to bring the right memories into an agent session and inspect why a memory appeared.
parents.title: Recall

children.title: What recall does
children.description: Learn what recall is matching and why it returns a small set of candidate memories.

children.title: Use recall in chat
children.description: Read the recall block as memory pointers and open useful memories before relying on them.

children.title: Read recall results
children.description: Interpret scores, candidates, query details, and usage state in the Recall UI.

children.title: Improve recall quality
children.description: Tune glossary terms, disclosure triggers, priority, and memory shape.

parents.description: Run Dream to review, clean up, and improve long-term memories with human approval.
parents.title: Dream

children.title: What Dream does
children.description: Understand Dream as a memory maintenance assistant.

children.title: Run Dream safely
children.description: Run Dream manually, review output, and keep maintenance controlled.

children.title: Review changes
children.description: Evaluate Dream suggestions by scope, trigger quality, glossary terms, safety, and traceability.

parents.description: Navigate the Lore Web console for memories, recall analysis, Dream runs, setup, and settings.
parents.title: Web Console

children.title: Main pages
children.description: Learn what each main page in the Lore Web console is used for.

children.title: Recommended routine
children.description: Follow a simple routine for memory review, recall checks, Dream, and backups.

children.title: What to check first
children.description: Open the right Web page based on the symptom you are seeing.

parents.description: Install Lore, start the Web console, and complete the first-run setup.
parents.title: Installation

children.title: Install Lore
children.description: Run the installer and choose the install mode that matches your environment.

children.title: First-run setup
children.description: Configure model endpoints, boot memories, settings, and runtime restarts.

children.title: Verify the install
children.description: Check the Web console, health status, setup completion, recall, and agent connection.

parents.description: Connect Codex, Claude Code, Pi, OpenClaw, Hermes, or any MCP client to the same Lore memory graph.
parents.title: Connect Agents

children.title: Choose runtimes
children.description: Pick the agent runtimes that should share the same Lore memory graph.

children.title: Connect an agent
children.description: Use the installer with selected channels, server URL, and API token.

children.title: What agents receive
children.description: Understand boot memory, recall candidates, MCP tools, and usage tracking.

parents.description: Configure the minimum settings that affect daily use: model endpoints, recall behavior, Dream, backup, and auth.
parents.title: Configuration

children.title: Essential settings
children.description: Set server URL, auth, embedding, View LLM, and backup basics.

children.title: Recall settings
children.description: Tune display threshold, item count, match weights, query limits, and timeout.

children.title: Dream and backup
children.description: Choose safe Dream and backup defaults before heavier memory maintenance.

parents.description: Follow practical workflows for writing memories, checking recall, running maintenance, and keeping Lore useful.
parents.title: Playbooks

children.title: Write a durable memory
children.description: Save one useful memory with path, priority, disclosure, glossary, and content.

children.title: Check recall quality
children.description: Run realistic queries and inspect why memories appear or stay hidden.

children.title: Clean up old memory
children.description: Split, move, delete, and review memories that have become stale or broad.

children.title: Back up memory
children.description: Create and verify a backup before heavier maintenance, migration, or broad memory cleanup.

children.title: Prepare a new project
children.description: Create a project memory branch with overview, commands, constraints, and glossary terms.

parents.description: Fix common setup, recall, Dream, login, and agent-connection issues.

children.title: Setup issues
children.description: Check server address, setup status, health, and auth problems.

children.title: Recall issues
children.description: Fix missing memories, noisy results, slow recall, and confusing query output.

children.title: Agent issues
children.description: Fix boot context, recall hooks, MCP tools, and stale agent memory.
